title: Chocolate Swirl Banana Bread
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no animal-derived ingredients. The bananas, olive oil, honey, oat milk, flours, cocoa powder, walnuts, and other baking ingredients contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active is false because this is primarily a sweet quick bread rather than a fiber-forward dish specifically structured to support LDL lowering. It uses some favorable ingredients like whole wheat flour, walnuts, and olive oil, but the overall format is still a baked treat rather than an actively LDL-lowering meal.
intro: Banana bread is one of those home-baked standards that has traveled easily across regions and generations, evolving with what is on hand while keeping its core appeal: ripe bananas, a tender crumb, and deep, familiar sweetness. This version leans into that tradition with a chocolate swirl that gives the loaf a marbled look and a richer flavor without losing its everyday character. Whole wheat flour adds a little structure, walnuts bring texture, and the result lands somewhere between a breakfast loaf, a snack cake, and a classic coffee companion.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Pan and Oven
items: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Lightly grease a 9 x 5-inch loaf pan with olive oil or line it with parchment paper.

stepGroups.title: Build the Banana Batter
items: Mash the bananas in a large bowl until mostly smooth. Add the olive oil, honey, oat milk, and vanilla extract and whisk until fully blended.

stepGroups.title: Mix the Dry Ingredients
items: In a separate bowl, combine the whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon. Add the dry mixture to the banana mixture and stir just until no dry streaks remain. Do not overmix.

stepGroups.title: Make the Chocolate Batter
items: Divide the batter evenly between two bowls. To one bowl, add the cocoa powder, honey, and oat milk. Stir until smooth and evenly combined.

stepGroups.title: Layer the Loaf
items: Fold the chopped walnuts into the plain batter. Spoon alternating scoops of the plain batter and the chocolate batter into the prepared loaf pan. Drag a knife through the batter in a few figure-eight motions to create a swirl without overblending the layers.

stepGroups.title: Bake and Cool
items: Bake for 55 to 65 minutes, until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean, allowing for a few moist chocolate streaks. Cool the loaf in the pan for 15 minutes, then transfer it to a rack to cool further before slicing.
